facts.title: divide() is the universal asserting divisor for every TP shard size
facts.fact_full:
Source: vllm/distributed/utils.py:L60-L64. ```python
def divide(numerator, denominator):
    assert numerator % denominator == 0, ...
    return numerator // denominator
``` Every shard size in vLLM goes through this function: `output_size_per_partition` (linear.py:L454), `input_size_per_partition` (linear.py:L1447), `num_heads` (linear.py:L1030), `num_kv_heads` (linear.py:L1035), `num_kv_head_replicas` (linear.py:L1033). It is THE contract that `tp_size | total_heads`, `tp_size | hidden_size`, etc. ŌĆö enforced at construction, not deep in the GEMM. If this assert fires, the model config is incompatible with the chosen tp_size.

facts.title: TP group is a module-level singleton _TP, retrieved via get_tp_group()
facts.fact_full: Source: vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py:L1494-L1592 (initialize_model_parallel sets `_TP`); parallel_state.py:L1229-L1235 (`get_tp_group` retrieves it). `_TP` is a `GroupCoordinator` that wraps a `torch.distributed.ProcessGroup`. There is no per-layer TP context; layers call `get_tp_group()` directly. `tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce` (communication_op.py:L12-L14) is literally `return get_tp_group().all_reduce(input_)`. For testing: `ensure_model_parallel_initialized` (parallel_state.py:L1738-L1781) asserts the world is set up before any layer can be constructed. In our single-process simulation, we hold all `tp_size` ranks' state in `self.rank_states[r]` ŌĆö production has one process per rank.

facts.title: ColumnParallel's output_partition_sizes is a LIST when fused
facts.fact_full:
Source: vllm/model_executor/layers/linear.py:L455-L460. ```python
self.output_partition_sizes = [self.output_size_per_partition]
if hasattr(self, 'output_sizes'):
    self.output_partition_sizes = [
        divide(s, tp_size) for s in self.output_sizes
    ]
``` The `output_sizes` attribute is set by subclasses (`MergedColumnParallelLinear`, `QKVParallelLinear`) BEFORE calling `super().__init__()` so the parent's init can read it via `hasattr`. This MRO trick is what lets one matmul produce multiple sharded outputs. Critical for the loader: each segment in `output_sizes` is sharded INDEPENDENTLY along the output dim (linear.py:L767-L820). A naive narrow on the fused output would put `[gate_rank0, gate_rank1, ŌĆ”]` in rank 0 instead of `[gate_rank0_shard, up_rank0_shard]`. We hit this bug in the first draft of `column_parallel.py:load_weight` and fixed it. Tester confirmed (T09): a test that reproduces the naive narrow against the proper per-segment loader shows ~7.7e-4 max-abs-diff at tp=4 with seed=0, small-magnitude weights (├Ś0.02). The proper loader achieves ~1e-7 vs the unsharded reference; the ratio is 4-5 orders of magnitude ŌĆö the bug is trivially detectable in any end-to-end correctness test, but only LATENT shows up if you skip the cross-rank comparison and only check forward shapes. This is what made the bug nasty in the first draft.

facts.title: GQA ├Ś TP ŌĆö KV head replication branch when tp_size >= total_num_kv_heads
facts.fact_full:
Source: vllm/model_executor/layers/linear.py:L1031-L1036. ```python
if tp_size >= self.total_num_kv_heads:
    self.num_kv_heads = 1
    self.num_kv_head_replicas = divide(tp_size, self.total_num_kv_heads)
else:
    self.num_kv_heads = divide(self.total_num_kv_heads, tp_size)
    self.num_kv_head_replicas = 1
``` For Llama-3-70B (8 KV heads): tp_size=8 gives one KV head per rank, clean 8├Ś memory savings; tp_size=16 gives one KV head per rank still, but replicated 2├Ś across rank-pairs. KV memory savings cap at 8├Ś regardless of tp_size. Trap-D in Ch08. In llama.py:L147-L155, the same logic appears in plain Python with a different shape: `assert tp_size % self.total_num_kv_heads == 0` when `total_num_kv_heads < tp_size` (the replication case must divide cleanly into rank groups).

facts.title: vLLM's all-reduce is a 1-line wrapper; the algorithm choice is in NCCL
facts.fact_full: Source: vllm/distributed/communication_op.py:L12-L14 + parallel_state.py:L502-L530. The user-facing `tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce(input_)` calls `get_tp_group().all_reduce(input_)`, which (after a `world_size==1` bypass) dispatches to `device_communicator.all_reduce(input_)`. The device communicator is NCCL on CUDA (device_communicators/cuda_communicator.py), which selects ring / tree / double-binary-tree internally based on payload size and topology. For ╬▒-╬▓ reasoning: NCCL ring is asymptotically `2*(P-1)/P ├Ś (╬▒ + (S/P) ├Ś ╬▓)`, which is what production vLLM hits at large payloads. Small payloads run on `custom_all_reduce.py` (NVLink P2P) when available ŌĆö that's the 'fast path' that beats ╬▒-bound regime.
